text: This KE describes a decrease in intratesticular testosterone production leading to a decrease in circulating levels of testosterone. Intratesticular testosterone can be measured in whole testicular tissue samples or by testing ex vivo testicular testosterone production, and circulating testosterone is measured in plasma or serum. In males, the testes produce and secrete the majority of the circulating testosterone, with only a small contribution from the adrenal gland (Naamneh Elzenaty et al., 2022). In mammals, intratesticular testosterone levels are 30- to 100-fold higher than serum testosterone levels (Coviello et al., 2004; McLachlan et al., 2002; Turner et al., 1984). Reducing testicular testosterone will consequently lead to a reduction in circulating levels as well.
